purpose: Pre-reg-required exclusion log (exclusion_log_path in exp1_pre_registration.json). Logs servers dropped from the doc-density-stratified frame post-vetting, with the pre-stated reason. score_every_one=true / drop_post_hoc=false from the pre-reg is honored at the VETTING stage (all 30 structurally-valid candidates were passed to the mechanical extraction script, none pre-filtered); this log covers exclusions discovered AT the mechanical-scoring stage.

new_exclusion_reason_added.definition: Mechanical tool-definition extraction (agentgauge/exp1_tool_def_extractor.py) returned zero tools for a server known, from the manual structural-validity vetting pass (approx_tool_count field, exp1_server_frame.json v1), to have >=5 tools. This is a MISSING-DATA outcome from an extraction-method gap (unsupported source pattern), NOT evidence the server is genuinely near-empty on documentation. Scoring these as composite_score=0.0 and tiering them 'near_empty' would inject false signal into the doc-density stratification -- excluded instead.
new_exclusion_reason_added.note: The original pre-reg's valid_exclusion_reasons (no_source / unrunnable / duplicate) did not anticipate a mechanical-extraction-method gap distinct from 'unrunnable'. This is a genuine amendment, flagged here and in exp1_pre_registration.json's amendments log, not silently absorbed into 'unrunnable' (the stub server was never built -- extraction failed before that step).

excluded.detail: 0 tools extracted via regex_best_effort; vetting recorded approx_tool_count=14, doc_quality=polished. No C-language tool-registration pattern is implemented in the extractor (regex patterns cover MCP TS SDK, mark3labs/mcp-go, and generic JS/JSON object literals only).

0 tools extracted via regex_best_effort; vetting recorded approx_tool_count=13, doc_quality=polished. Go pattern in the extractor targets mark3labs/mcp-go's mcp.NewTool(...)/mcp.WithDescription(...) idiom specifically (confirmed working on matlab-mcp-server and github-mcp-server, both Go); this repo evidently uses a different Go MCP SDK or registration style not covered.

0 tools extracted via regex_best_effort; vetting recorded approx_tool_count=13. Manually flagged doc_quality=thin during vetting -- genuinely low description density is plausible here, but 0/13 is inconsistent with 'thin' (thin implies short descriptions exist, not zero extractable schema) and no verbatim inspection was done to confirm vs. an extraction miss. Excluded as extraction_failed rather than assumed near_empty -- would need a source read to resolve, out of scope for this pass.

0 tools extracted; vetting recorded approx_tool_count=82. CONFIRMED via direct inspection (git ls-tree): tools are declared as YAML manifest files under manifests/tools/*.yaml, not in TS source at all -- a genuinely different, repo-specific tool-declaration architecture. Not a generalizable extractor gap; explicitly out of scope (would require a bespoke YAML-manifest parser for this one repo's convention).

0 tools extracted; vetting recorded approx_tool_count=225, doc_quality=moderate. No Java tool-registration pattern is implemented in the extractor at all (Java MCP servers likely use annotation-based registration, e.g. Spring AI-style @Tool, not covered by any existing regex).
0 tools extracted; vetting recorded approx_tool_count=25, doc_quality=polished. Same Java coverage gap as bethington-ghidra-mcp.

0 tools extracted; vetting recorded approx_tool_count=250, doc_quality=polished. Language was never determined during discovery (recorded 'unknown'); extractor fell through to the generic non-Python regex path, which did not match this repo's convention.

ADDED 2026-07-04 (v3 correction): initially scored composite=25.0 on 2 tools ('list_tools', 'call_tool') that were NEVER real domain tools -- they are the low-level MCP SDK's own @server.list_tools()/@server.call_tool() protocol-handler registration methods, false-positived by _has_tool_decorator's substring-'tool' check (fixed, see agentgauge/exp1_mirror.py). CONFIRMED via direct source inspection: this server's real ~10-tool catalog is returned dynamically by tools.get_tool_schemas(), which aggregates from a runtime registry (self._tools dict populated elsewhere) -- not itself AST-extractable without deep, repo-specific call-graph tracing. Excluded as extraction_failed, not scored as a 2-tool near-empty server.

not_excluded_diagnostic_fixes_applied: sec-edgar-mcp (Python): initially 0 tools -- AST decorator extraction found nothing because this repo registers tools imperatively (mcp.add_tool(fn) over a plain function list, no decorator). Fixed by extending agentgauge/exp1_tool_def_extractor.py to also resolve this idiom; recovered 21 tools, composite=80.1.

LycheeMem (Python): initially 0 tools -- tools are declared as a module-level TOOLS_SCHEMA = [{'name':..., 'description':...}] dict literal, not decorators. Fixed by falling back to the existing JSON-literal regex (already used for non-Python sources) when Python AST finds nothing; recovered 4 tools, composite=100.0.

aws-iam-mcp anchor (Python, awslabs/mcp monorepo): initially 0 tools -- the anchor loader hardcoded language='unknown' for both validation anchors regardless of actual language, and scored the ENTIRE monorepo rather than the pre-registered src/iam-mcp-server subpath. Fixed: anchors now carry explicit language + source_subpath fields; recovered 29 tools, composite=100.0.

blazickjp-arxiv-mcp-server (Python): ADDED 2026-07-04 (v3) -- initially 71.8/2-tools, computed on phantom tools bled in from mcp.types.Prompt(...)/PromptArgument(...) definitions (a DIFFERENT MCP primitive, same name/description shape as a real Tool()). Fixed by adding a precise Tool(name=..., description=...) constructor-call pattern anchored on the literal 'Tool' identifier, tried before the generic fallback. Recovered 8 real tools, composite=100.0 (near_empty -> well_documented).

lucasastorian-llmwiki (Python): ADDED 2026-07-04 (v3) -- initially 25.0/0%-described, because agentgauge.exp1_mirror.extract_python_tools read ONLY the function's own docstring, never a decorator keyword argument like @mcp.tool(name=..., description=...) -- FastMCP's own officially-documented kwarg style, and this repo's actual convention for all 13 tools. Fixed: decorator kwarg now takes precedence over docstring (matching FastMCP's runtime behavior). Real: 92.3% described, composite=78.1 -- this server was NOT genuinely near-empty; it was a measurement artifact.

summary.shortfall_vs_pre_registered_freshly_samp: 28 - 22 = 6 short; escalated to GG as an open decision (see exp1_pre_registration.json amendments), not silently resolved.
summary.v3_correction_note: vitali87-code-graph-rag moved from 'scoreable/near_empty' to 'excluded/extraction_failed' on 2026-07-04, after 3 confirmed extraction bugs were found and fixed (see exp1_server_frame.json bug_fixes_this_version). Two other scoreable servers had composite scores corrected in place (see not_excluded_diagnostic_fixes_applied): blazickjp-arxiv-mcp-server and lucasastorian-llmwiki.
